raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.3": 4,
    "R1.4": 4,
    "R1.7": 4,
    "RX.1": 4,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 4
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The revised draft flags at least one claim the evidence does not support (e.g., the '21% relative improvement' over JPICE on Oxford, which is a misleading comparison).",
    "R1.4": "The revised draft names one prior work and articulates the delta between it and the new method.",
    "R1.7": "The revised draft audits every load-bearing citation; flags missing key works with bibliographic precision.",
    "RX.1": "The revised draft marks uncertainty for clearly out-of-reach claims (e.g., the 21% relative improvement over JPICE on Oxford).",
    "RX.3": "The revised draft output covers exactly the agreed J*(d), with no substantial drift from the requested scope.",
    "RX.4": "The revised draft is harm-free but with one minor regression (the misleading comparison to JPICE on Oxford).",
    "RX.5": "The revised draft annotates every non-cosmetic change with source and reason, although some minor edits are not annotated."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.3": 0.9,
    "R1.4": 0.8,
    "R1.7": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.85,
    "RX.3": 0.95,
    "RX.4": 0.8,
    "RX.5": 0.85
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The revised draft flags at least one claim the evidence does not support (e.g., the '21% relative improvement' over JPICE on Oxford, which is a misleading comparison).
rationale.R1.4: The revised draft names one prior work and articulates the delta between it and the new method.
rationale.R1.7: The revised draft audits every load-bearing citation; flags missing key works with bibliographic precision.
rationale.RX.1: The revised draft marks uncertainty for clearly out-of-reach claims (e.g., the 21% relative improvement over JPICE on Oxford).
rationale.RX.3: The revised draft output covers exactly the agreed J*(d), with no substantial drift from the requested scope.
rationale.RX.4: The revised draft is harm-free but with one minor regression (the misleading comparison to JPICE on Oxford).
rationale.RX.5: The revised draft annotates every non-cosmetic change with source and reason, although some minor edits are not annotated.
